Add 162 unit tests covering critical and medium priority gaps

Test files added (17 new files, 2708 lines):

Critical - DSL Router:
- RoutePredicatesTest (24 tests): fieldEquals, fieldMatches, senderEquals,
  senderMatches, idEquals, idMatches, any, and/or/negate composition
- RouteHandlersTest (10 tests): forward, multicast, forwardWithPriority,
  priority, drop, dropQuietly, dropToDlq
- RacerFunctionalRouterTest (8 tests): evaluate first-match-wins, no-match
  PASS, defaultRoute, predicate exceptions, builder immutability

Critical - Publishers:
- MessageEnvelopeBuilderTest (14 tests): all 6 build method overloads,
  payload serialization, routed flag, traceparent, UUID generation
- RacerChannelPublisherImplTest (10 tests): pub/sub and durable stream
  paths, rate limiting, schema validation, routed flag, sync blocking
- RacerShardedStreamPublisherTest (12 tests): CRC-16 routing, hash ring,
  failover retry, allShardKeys, deterministic sharding

Critical - Utils/Health:
- RacerHealthIndicatorTest (8 tests): Redis ping up/down, DLQ depth,
  consumer lag threshold, optional dependency handling
- RacerChannelResolverTest (11 tests): placeholder resolution, channelRef
  vs channel, streamKeyRef vs streamKey, fallback behavior
- RacerStreamUtilsTest (10 tests): ensureGroup, BUSYGROUP exception chain
  detection, ackRecord, nested cause handling

Medium - Controllers:
- DlqControllerTest (7 tests): all 5 DLQ REST endpoints + stats
- SchemaControllerTest (9 tests): schema disabled, list, get, validate
- RouterControllerTest (4 tests): rule listing, dry-run routing
- RetentionControllerTest (2 tests): config retrieval, trim execution

Medium - Registrars:
- RacerPollRegistrarTest (9 tests): bean discovery, CronMatcher, lifecycle
- RacerStreamListenerRegistrarTest (9 tests): bean discovery, tracked
  groups, counters, interceptors, lifecycle
- RacerResponderRegistrarTest (5 tests): pub/sub and stream discovery

Medium - Services:
- RacerRetentionServiceTest (10 tests): stream trimming, DLQ pruning,
  expired entry filtering, error resilience, config accessors

Total: 405 tests passing (243 existing + 162 new), 0 failures.

Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > [aw] No-Op Runs > This issue tracks all no-op runs from agentic workflows in this repository. Each workflow run that completes with a no-op message (indicating no action was needed) posts a comment here. > >
> 📘 What is a No-Op? > > A no-op (no operation) occurs when an agentic workflow runs successfully but determines that no action is required. For example: > - A security scanner that finds no issues > - An update checker that finds nothing to update > - A monitoring workflow that finds everything is healthy > > These are successful outcomes, not failures, and help provide transparency into workflow behavior. > >
> >
> 🎯 How This Helps > > This issue helps you: > - Track workflows that ran but determined no action was needed > - Distinguish between failures and intentional no-ops > - Monitor workflow health by seeing when workflows decide not to act > >

commit.message:
fix(L-3/M-3): synchronize circuit breaker and DLQ handler; add tuner + cluster tests

L-3 fix: RacerCircuitBreaker.recordOutcome() is now synchronized so
that window + failureCount updates are atomic under concurrent calls,
preventing failure-rate drift when multiple threads record outcomes
simultaneously on the same sliding window.

M-3 fix: AbstractRacerRegistrar.getDeadLetterHandler() is now
synchronized to prevent two concurrent first-callers both seeing null,
both resolving the ObjectProvider, and both writing the result back.
In practice Spring returns the same singleton, but the race was
unnecessary and confusing.

AdaptiveConcurrencyTunerTest (12 tests): covers AIMD additive
increase, multiplicative decrease on >10% error rate, subtract-one
on throughput drop, stable-throughput no-change, max/min boundary
enforcement, semaphore acquire/release, shutdown idempotency, and
ResizableSemaphore permit manipulation.

RacerConsistentHashRingTest: added constructor_rejectsNegativeShards
and getShardFor_distributionIsRoughlyUniform (per-shard 10–40%
bound over 1000 keys with 200 virtual nodes per shard).

Test count: 229 → 243 (+14)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add xcind-proxy, workspace mode, and hook system (#3)

* Add PRD for xcind-proxy feature

Draft product requirements document covering the proxy auto-generation
feature, .xcind/ directory convention, SHA-based caching, and the
post-resolve hook system.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Sb6GapQewWFc7jWsPoZc6C

* Update PRD hook design: env var data passing, cache artifacts, and cache-miss-only hooks

Redesign the hook system in the xcind-proxy PRD:
- Rename hook point from post-resolve to post-resolve-generate
- Pass pipeline data via env vars (XCIND_SHA, XCIND_CACHE_DIR, XCIND_GENERATED_DIR)
- Define concrete cache artifacts (config.json, resolved-config.yaml)
- Broaden hook stdout contract from -f only to any Docker Compose flags
- Only call hooks on cache miss; persist and replay hook output on cache hit
- Always pre-compute docker compose config and cache the result

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Scind proxy research doc and augment xcind-proxy PRD

Distill all proxy-related information from the Scind spec into a
self-contained research document. Update the PRD to fill three gaps:
Docker label definitions, proxy infrastructure lifecycle (init/up/down/
status), and the override generation algorithm. Also switches service
discovery from compose labels to .xcind.sh declarations, adds hostname
generation spec with .localhost zero-config DNS, and adds global config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add __xcind-render-template function and update PRD

Add a loop-based template substitution function to xcind-lib.bash that
replaces {key} placeholders with provided values. Update the proxy PRD
to document template rendering (section 4.5) and reference it in
hostname generation (section 8) and the override algorithm (section 12).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Align xcind-proxy PRD with Scind naming conventions

- Adopt broad-to-narrow hostname ordering: {app}-{export}.{domain}
- Add export name vs compose service name distinction (= separator)
- Add -http protocol suffix to router names
- Add xcind.export.* labels for service discovery
- Replace {service} template variable with {export} throughout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add workspace discovery, URL template system, and hybrid template generation to PRD

Introduces lightweight workspace discovery from directory structure (two-level
.xcind.sh hierarchy), configurable URL templates with workspaceless/workspace
variants, and a hybrid template approach for compose override generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add workspace networking design to xcind-proxy PRD

Extends the PRD with per-workspace internal networks ({workspace}-internal)
for inter-app communication via Docker network aliases. All compose services
get aliases (not just exports), using a configurable template. Adds workspace
hook (xcind-workspace-hook), override generation algorithm, and success criteria.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix PRD consistency issues, design gaps, and implementation risks

PRD changes:
- C1: Update SHA formula to include .xcind.sh and global proxy config
- C2: Add export grouping step to prevent duplicate YAML keys
- C4: Clarify hooks must be sourced bash functions, not external scripts
- D1: Revert XCIND_WORKSPACE_SERVICE_TEMPLATE default to {app}-{service}
- S1: Add project name field to generated compose files
- S2: Add XCIND_IS_WORKSPACE guard for workspace-root detection
- S3: Skip proxy generation when XCIND_PROXY_EXPORTS is empty
- S4: Gate cache/hook pipeline behind hook registration check
- S5: Verify referenced files exist on cache hit
- S6: Default XCIND_PROXY_DOMAIN before sourcing global config
- I1: Fix scind-proxy typo in Section 9
- I2: Soften "no branching" claim in Section 5.5

Code changes:
- C3: Fix __xcind-load-config to preserve workspace-set values
- C5: Add yq to Dockerfile, flake.nix devShell, and Nix package

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add workspace discovery, SHA caching, hook system, and xcind-proxy CLI

Phase 1 of xcind-proxy implementation:

- Workspace discovery (__xcind-discover-workspace) from parent .xcind.sh
- __xcind-app-root skips directories with XCIND_IS_WORKSPACE=1
- Late-bind workspace self-declaration for standalone apps
- App name resolution and URL template selection (workspaceless/workspace)
- SHA-based caching (__xcind-compute-sha, __xcind-populate-cache)
- Hook system (__xcind-run-hooks) with cache hit/miss and output replay
- 13-step pipeline in xcind-compose (backward compatible — steps 9-12
  only run when XCIND_HOOKS_POST_RESOLVE_GENERATE is non-empty)
- xcind-proxy CLI with init/up/down/status subcommands
- 94 tests passing (28 new)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add proxy and workspace hook libraries with tests

Phase 2 of xcind-proxy implementation:

- xcind-proxy-lib.bash: xcind-proxy-hook generates compose.proxy.yaml
  from XCIND_PROXY_EXPORTS declarations with Traefik labels, hostname
  generation, port inference, service validation, and multi-export grouping
- xcind-workspace-lib.bash: xcind-workspace-hook generates
  compose.workspace.yaml with network aliases for all compose services
- Hybrid template approach for YAML generation via __xcind-render-template
- Export entry parsing (4 formats: web, api:3000, db=postgres, db=postgres:5432)
- 76 proxy/workspace tests passing
- Updated contrib/test-all to include new files in shellcheck and test runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace shasum with cross-platform __xcind-sha256 wrapper

shasum (a Perl script) is unavailable on Alpine/Ubuntu without
perl-utils. Add a small wrapper that prefers sha256sum (Linux
coreutils/busybox) and falls back to shasum (macOS stock).
Also add missing shellcheck SC1091 disable for dynamic source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Restructure examples into workspaceless/ and workspaces/ with real content

Move acmeapps and advanced into examples/workspaceless/ and add supporting
files (compose configs, env files, source code) so they actually run.
Add a new workspace example at examples/workspaces/dev/ with frontend
(nginx) and backend (node + postgres) apps demonstrating proxy exports
and workspace hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix xcind-compose silent exit when no hooks are registered

XCIND_HOOKS_POST_RESOLVE_GENERATE was never initialized as an empty
array. With set -u, the unbound reference on the guard line caused an
immediate silent exit — 2>/dev/null hid the error message but could
not prevent termination. Initialize the array in xcind-lib.bash and
remove the now-unnecessary stderr redirect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix workspace hook leaking docker network ID into compose command

docker network create prints the network ID to stdout, which was
captured as hook output and appended to XCIND_DOCKER_COMPOSE_OPTS,
causing "unknown docker command" errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix examples

* Fix hook overlays dropping default network and xcind-config missing hook files

Hook-generated compose overlays (proxy, workspace) added explicit networks to
services, which caused Docker Compose to silently drop the implicit default
network. Added `default: {}` to every service's networks in both hook templates
so the project default network is preserved alongside xcind-proxy and
workspace-internal networks.

xcind-config only ran app-root detection and config loading, skipping the full
pipeline (workspace discovery, URL templates, SHA, cache, hooks). Aligned it
with xcind-compose so --preview, JSON, and --files outputs all include
hook-generated overlay files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update README with workspace, hooks, and proxy documentation

workspace mode, the hook system, xcind-proxy command, proxy exports,

updates the file structure tree to include all current files.

and xcind-config, leaving xcind-proxy and the newer library files out of the
installation. This adds all three missing artifacts and wraps xcind-proxy in

* Fix xcind-proxy network creation silently swallowing errors

The `docker network create ... 2>/dev/null || true` pattern suppressed
all errors, causing init and up to report success even when the network
wasn't actually created. Replace with an ensure-network helper that
checks existence first and fails loudly on creation errors. Add
`xcind-proxy up --force` to recover from broken state by tearing down
containers and recreating the network.

* Suppress docker network create hash output in xcind-proxy init

stdout on success. Redirect stdout to /dev/null so the hash doesn't
leak into user-facing output, while still preserving stderr for errors.

* Fix Traefik routing to multi-network containers by adding traefik.docker.network label

When a proxied service is on both the default and xcind-proxy networks,
Traefik can pick the wrong network IP to route to, causing upstream
connection failures. Adding traefik.docker.network=xcind-proxy to all
service templates ensures Traefik always uses the correct network.

Add traefik.http.routers.{router}.service={router} to all templates so each

commit.message:
Fix pk_range_cache to use .item_by_rid() for correct URL fetching (#4032)

Fixes two issues in
`sdk/cosmos/azure_data_cosmos/src/routing/partition_key_range_cache.rs`:

## Changes Made

### Primary fix — correct pkranges resource link URL construction

In `get_routing_map_for_collection()`, changed `.item(collection_rid)`
to `.item_by_rid(collection_rid)`. The `.item()` method URL-encodes the
value via `LinkSegment::new()`, so RIDs like `pLLZAIuPigw=` were being
encoded to `pLLZAIuPigw%3D` in the URL, causing Cosmos DB to return 404
on every partition key range fetch. The `.item_by_rid()` method uses
`LinkSegment::identity()` (no encoding), producing the correct URL:

```
dbs/perfdb/colls/pLLZAIuPigw=/pkranges    ← correct
```

### Error logging in try_lookup

Added `tracing::warn!` before the `.ok()` call in `try_lookup()` that
was silently swallowing errors. Routing map fetch failures now emit a
warning with the `collection_rid` and error details, making silent
failures visible in diagnostics.

### Unit tests

Added three unit tests in `partition_key_range_cache.rs` that directly
verify the URL construction behavior:

- `pkranges_link_rid_with_equals_is_not_encoded` — verifies
`item_by_rid()` preserves `=` in the RID path (e.g., `pLLZAIuPigw=` →
`dbs/perfdb/colls/pLLZAIuPigw=/pkranges`)
- `pkranges_link_item_encodes_equals_incorrectly` — documents the bug:
`item()` encodes `=` to `%3D`, producing a path that causes 404s
- `pkranges_link_rid_with_plus_is_not_encoded` — verifies
`item_by_rid()` also preserves `+` and `/` in base64 RIDs

## Root Cause

PR #4005 changed the `pk_range_cache` key from container name to
collection RID. The URL construction code was not updated to use
`.item_by_rid()`, causing RID URL-encoding and subsequent 404s on every
pkranges fetch. Because errors were silently swallowed and `AsyncCache`
does not cache errors, this failed on every single request, resulting in
write lock contention and loss of client-side partition key routing (~7%
throughput regression observed in benchmarks).

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


----

*This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

<issue_title>[Cosmos] pk_range_cache uses .item() instead of
.item_by_rid() causing silent 404s on every request</issue_title>
<issue_description>## Bug Report

### Summary

PR [#4005](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-rust/pull/4005)
changed the `pk_range_cache` key from container **name** to collection
**RID**. However, the code that constructs the URL for fetching
partition key ranges still uses `.item(collection_rid)` instead of
`.item_by_rid(collection_rid)`. This causes the RID to be URL-encoded
(e.g., `=` → `%3D`), resulting in a **404 from Cosmos DB** on every
partition key range fetch attempt.

Because `try_lookup` silently swallows errors via `Ok(routing_map.ok())`
and `AsyncCache` does not cache errors, this failure repeats on **every
single request**, causing:

1. **1.6M extra 404 requests/hour** observed on a benchmark account
after deploying the change
2. **Write lock contention** in `AsyncCache` as every concurrent
operation serializes through the failed fetch path
3. **Loss of client-side partition key routing** — the gateway must
route all requests instead
4. **~7% throughput regression** observed in continuous benchmarks (110M
→ 102M requests/hour)

### Root Cause (3-step chain)

#### Step 1: Wrong URL encoding — `.item()` vs `.item_by_rid()`

In `partition_key_range_cache.rs`, `get_routing_map_for_collection()`:

```rust
let pk_range_link = self
    .database_link                       // dbs/perfdb
    .feed(ResourceType::Containers)
    .item(collection_rid)                // ← BUG: .item() URL-encodes the RID
    .feed(ResourceType::PartitionKeyRanges);
```

`.item()` calls `LinkSegment::new()` which URL-encodes the value. RIDs
like `pLLZAIuPigw=` get the `=` encoded to `%3D`:
```
dbs/perfdb/colls/pLLZAIuPigw%3D/pkranges  ← Cosmos DB returns 404
```

Should use `.item_by_rid()` which calls `LinkSegment::identity()` (no
encoding):
```
dbs/perfdb/colls/pLLZAIuPigw=/pkranges    ← correct
```

#### Step 2: Error silently swallowed

In `partition_key_range_cache.rs` line 147, `try_lookup()`:

```rust
Ok(routing_map.ok())  // Converts Err(404) → Ok(None), invisible to caller
```

The caller in `container_connection.rs` sees `Ok(None)` and skips the
routing block entirely:

```rust
let routing_map = self.pk_range_cache.try_lookup(collection_rid, None).await?;
if let Some(routing_map) = routing_map {
    // SKIPPED — no client-side partition key range resolution
}
```

#### Step 3: Errors not cached → retried on every request

`AsyncCache::get()` only caches successful values. When `compute()`
returns `Err`, the error propagates and the cache remains empty. Every
subsequent request:

1. Read lock → cache miss
2. Acquire **write lock** (serializes all concurrent operations on the
same key)
3. HTTP request to Cosmos DB → **404**
4. Error propagated, cache stays empty
5. Error swallowed as `Ok(None)`
6. Routing bypassed

### Evidence from Benchmarks

Continuous benchmark on `cosmos-perf-rg` (4 pods, concurrency=100, 400K
RU/s):

| Hour (UTC) | 404 Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 13:02 – 17:02 | 3,500 – 6,300 | Normal background |
| **18:02** | **1,645,604** | After deploying commit `98d01c8` |

Throughput dropped from ~110M req/hr to ~102M req/hr (~7% regression).
Server-side latency actually decreased (fewer effective requests
reaching the service), confirming the bottleneck is client-side.

### Suggested Fix

```diff
// partition_key_range_cache.rs, get_routing_map_for_collection()
let pk_range_link = self
    .database_link
    .feed(ResourceType::Containers)
-   .item(collection_rid)
+   .item_by_rid(collection_rid)
    .feed(ResourceType::PartitionKeyRanges);
```

Additionally, consider:
- Logging errors in `try_lookup` before swallowing them, to make silent
failures visible
- Adding negative caching (or a backoff) in `AsyncCache` to avoid
retrying failed fetches on every request

### Affected Version

Commit
[`98d01c8`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-rust/commit/98d01c8)
on `release/azure_data_cosmos-previews` branch (PR
Azure/azure-sdk-for-rust#4005).
</issue_description>

perf: batched sensitivity analysis with jax.vmap (#153)

Add vmap-based batched energy evaluation to JAX engines so that
compute_sensitivity() can evaluate all 2N+1 parameter perturbations
in a single vectorized call instead of sequentially.

Engine layer:
- MMEngine base: supports_batched_energy() / batched_energy() with
  sequential fallback for non-JAX backends
- JaxEngine: vmap over _energy_fn with cached JIT compilation
- JaxMDEngine: vmap over _scalar_energy_fn, broadcasting coords
  and neighbor list

Objective layer:
- is_energy_only(): detect pure-energy reference sets
- batched_scores(): dispatch to batched or sequential path
- _batched_energy_scores(): vectorized residual computation

Cycling layer:
- compute_sensitivity() builds (2N+1, P) param matrix upfront
  and calls batched_scores() for the entire sensitivity sweep

Tests:
- 5 new JAX-specific tests covering batched_energy, is_energy_only,
  batched_scores consistency, and end-to-end compute_sensitivity

commit.message:
Enable verbose logging when System.Debug is set in Azure Pipelines (#4027)

When `System.Debug` is enabled in AzDO, pipeline tests run without any
enhanced logging, making it difficult to diagnose failures in the Rust
test code or the `test-proxy` process.

## Changes

- **`eng/pipelines/templates/variables/rust.yml`**: Update `RUST_LOG` to
use `trace` when `System.Debug == true`, and `info` otherwise
(previously `debug` / empty string). This is the single source of truth
for `RUST_LOG` across all pipelines.

- **`ci.tests.yml` / `live.tests.yml`**: Add a conditional step that
sets `Logging__LogLevel__Default=Debug` (ASP.NET Core log level for
`test-proxy`) when `System.Debug == true`. The step runs before the test
proxy starts and before tests execute so both processes inherit the
variable.

- **`ci.tests.yml`**: Add a "Dump Test Proxy Audit Logs" step (`GET
/Audit/Logs` via `Invoke-WebRequest`) conditioned on `System.Debug ==
true`, alongside the existing log dump step, with error handling so a
failure doesn't mask the original test result.

fix: enable containerd slow_chown for LXC on kernels < 5.19

On LXC containers with kernels older than 5.19, the overlayfs
snapshotter does not support idmap mounts. When KubeletInUserNamespace
is enabled, containerd attempts idmap mounts and fails with:

  snapshotter "overlayfs" doesn't support idmap mounts on this host,
  configure `slow_chown` to allow a slower and expensive fallback

Add a kernel version check (_kernel_supports_idmap) and, when the
kernel lacks support, write a K3s containerd config template that sets
slow_chown = true for the overlayfs snapshotter. This lets containerd
fall back to chown-based rootfs preparation instead of failing.

commit.message:
Ignore cargo2junit test-failure exit code in Convert-TestResultsToJUnit.ps1 (#3965)

`cargo2junit` exits non-zero when the converted test output contains
failures. Since CI already reports test failures in a separate task,
this exit code is redundant and causes the conversion step to fail
unnecessarily.

### Changes

Update `eng/scripts/Convert-TestResultsToJUnit.ps1` to use
`Start-Process` with `-RedirectStandardOutput` and
`-RedirectStandardError` to reliably capture stdout and stderr into
separate files. Stdout (JUnit XML) is written directly to the output
file. Stderr is always printed to CI logs for debugging visibility.

On non-zero exit from `cargo2junit`, filter out the known `"One or more
tests failed."` line from stderr using `-notlike`. Only treat the
conversion as failed if other error lines remain, so genuine conversion
errors are still caught while test-failure exit codes are ignored.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Convert-TestResultsToJUnit.ps1 should not treat test
failures as an error</issue_title>
> <issue_description>The `cargo2junit` tool currently exits with a
nonzero exit code if there is a test failure. Since CI runs tests and
JUnit conversion in separate tasks, this failure signal is redundant and
can be ignored. The script should only fail if conversion itself fails.
> 
> The current implementation of cargo2junit fails if a test failure is
detected:
> 
> ```rust
> fn determine_exit_code(report: &Report) -> Result<()> {
>     if report.testsuites().is_empty() {
> Err(Error::new(ErrorKind::NotFound, "No test suite results were
found.".to_owned()))
>     } else if report
>         .testsuites()
>         .iter()
>         .flat_map(|suite| suite.testcases().iter())
>         .any(|testcase| testcase.is_error() || testcase.is_failure())
>     {
>         Err(Error::other("One or more tests failed.".to_owned()))
>     } else {
>         Ok(())
>     }
> }
> ```
> 
> stderr in the logs includes the following: 
> 
> ```
> Error: Custom { kind: Other, error: "One or more tests failed." }
> ```
> 
> To prevent noise, use a different algorithm: 
> 
> * If cargo2junit exits 0: continue
> * If cargo2junit exits non-zero: 
> * If stderr only has the "One or more tests failed." error: continue
(do not treat as a failure)
> * If stderr contains any additional errors: treat as a failure and set
`$succeeded = $false`
> 
> Don't do any clever parsing of stderr, it's enough to assume that
there's one error per-line. Make sure that stderr logs are still printed
so that the CI logs contain whatever came out of stderr.. I want to be
able to debug in the event of a future break. </issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>Open a PR with these changes</agent_instructions>
>

primitives.

invoking opcodes.

Add validation step checklists to plan.md to prevent agents from skipping validation (#1499)

* Add validation steps to plan.md

We've seen a number of failures where the agent skips some or all of the listed validation steps. Here we try to mitigate this by creating a list of TODO items for each validation recipe and adding them to the .azure/plan.md document. This encourages the agent to complete all the items as part of working through the plan.

Example:

```markdown
### Phase 3: Validation
- [x] Invoke azure-validate skill
- [ ] All validation checks pass
  - [x] 1. AZD Installation
  - [x] 2. Schema Validation
  - [x] 3. Environment Setup
  - [x] 4. Authentication Check
  - [x] 5. Subscription/Location Check
  - [x] 6. Aspire Pre-Provisioning Checks
  - [x] 7. Provision Preview
  - [x] 8. Build Verification
  - [x] 9. Package Validation
  - [x] 10. Azure Policy Validation
  - [ ] 11. Aspire Post-Provisioning Checks
- [ ] Update plan status to "Validated"
```

* Bump azure-validate version to 1.0.2

commit.message:
32 issue 002d implementar novas apis prioritárias e documentá las no scalaropenapi (#33)

* feat(repositories): add read helpers and cotahist/market queries

Add related Pydantic models in app.schemas so pre-commit ty sees consistent imports.

* feat(api): add new endpoint for intraday OHLC candles and enhance existing candle retrieval

- Introduced a new route `/fact-quotes/{ticker}/candles` to aggregate intraday OHLC candles from `fact_quotes`, utilizing hypertable data.
- Enhanced the existing `/quotes/{ticker}/candles` endpoint to support multiple intervals and improved error handling for date validation.
- Updated schemas to include `CandleRead` for consistent data representation across candle endpoints.
- Added validation for interval and date formats to ensure robust API responses.

* feat(tests): enhance API and repository tests for new endpoints and functionality

- Added integration tests for new endpoints including `/fact-quotes/{ticker}/candles`, `/etl/runs`, and `/cotahist/{ticker}/history` to ensure proper response handling.
- Updated existing tests to verify the presence of new paths in the OpenAPI schema.
- Introduced unit tests for asset detail orchestration and candle bucketing, improving coverage for asset overview and technical indicators.
- Created a new integration test file for market data repositories, validating database interactions and data retrieval.

* feat(api): add asset coverage and overview endpoints

- Introduced new endpoints `/assets/{ticker}/coverage` and `/assets/{ticker}/overview` to provide detailed asset data coverage and consolidated overviews.
- Implemented corresponding use case functions `build_asset_coverage` and `build_asset_overview` to handle data retrieval and response formatting.
- Updated asset-related schemas to include `AssetCoverageRead` and `AssetOverviewRead` for structured API responses.
- Created a new module `asset_detail.py` to encapsulate asset detail orchestration logic.

* feat(api): add COTAHIST and market overview endpoints

- Introduced new API routes for COTAHIST data, including `/cotahist/{ticker}/history` and a filterable list endpoint.
- Added a market overview endpoint at `/market/overview` to provide aggregated metrics for trading days.
- Updated the main FastAPI application to include the new routers for COTAHIST and market functionalities.
- Enhanced API documentation to reflect the new endpoints and their descriptions.

* feat(quotes): implement OHLC candle generation and technical indicators

- Added `candles.py` to build OHLC candles from daily quotes and intraday points, including functions for daily and intraday candle aggregation.
- Introduced `indicators.py` for calculating technical indicators such as SMA, EMA, and RSI on close-price series.
- Implemented validation functions for interval and period inputs to ensure correct usage.
- Enhanced data handling with functions to clip candles chronologically and build indicator series from date and close-price lists.

* feat(api): enhance ETL run history endpoint and add market data constants

- Introduced a new endpoint `/etl/runs` to list ETL run history with pagination and filtering options for pipeline name, status, source date, and started_at window.
- Added helper functions to parse datetime filters and convert ETL run data into a structured response format.
- Created a new module `market_data_constants.py` to define constants for market-data APIs, including accepted candle intervals and indicator names, along with sensible bounds for indicator periods.

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): update route paths for COTAHIST and fact quotes endpoints

- Changed the route path for the COTAHIST history endpoint from an empty string to a trailing slash for consistency.
- Enhanced error handling in the fact quotes endpoint to raise an HTTPException when the requested range exceeds the allowed points limit, providing clearer guidance for users.

* fix(tests): correct COTAHIST endpoint path in integration test

- Updated the integration test for the OpenAPI schema to reflect the correct route path for the COTAHIST endpoint by adding a trailing slash.

* refactor(repository): update return type for get_latest_for_ticker method and enhance description in AssetOverviewRead schema

- Changed the return type of the get_latest_for_ticker method to indicate it can return a FactQuote or None.
- Improved the description of the latest_update_at field in the AssetOverviewRead schema for clarity on its purpose and data source.

* refactor(asset_detail): remove unused asset timestamps from build_asset_overview function

- Eliminated the extension of asset creation and update timestamps from the updates list in the build_asset_overview function, streamlining the code.
